content.text:
Data Warehousing and Data Preparation
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEF22  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Lab:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Data warehouses are currently the best response to the integration, storage, and personalised retrieval of large quantities of information from diverse sources. This course provides the foundations for designing, modelling, implementing, and maintaining data warehouses for decision information systems. It also covers data preparation and integration techniques to feed a data warehouse from heterogeneous sources.
Course Content (21h)
1. Introduction to Data Warehouses
From information systems to decision information systems
Motivations and definition of a data warehouse
Properties, objectives and functions of a data warehouse
2. Data Warehouse Architecture
Data warehouse architecture
Warehouse / Data Marts duality
Metadata and data quality
Data flow in a warehouse
OLAP and OLTP: definition, properties, operation, and comparison
3. Data Extraction and Integration
Integration of source schemas
Virtualised integration (mediation: GAV and LAV approaches)
Materialised integration (data warehouse)
The ETL process: data extraction, cleaning, and loading
4. Warehouse Modelling
Multidimensional model
General modelling approach
Fact table and dimension tables
Multidimensional model schemas: star schema, snowflake schema, constellation schema
Case study
5. Physical Data Placement Optimisation
Dedicated operators
Optimisation of join and aggregate operators
View materialisation
Storage medium selection
6. Querying Data Warehouses
Data warehouse management systems (DBMS for decision-making, etc.)
Multidimensional queries (complex SQL, etc.)
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — tests, personal work
Bibliography
Ralph Kimball (2013).
The Data Warehouse Toolkit: The Definitive Guide to Dimensional Modeling
. Wiley.
Paulraj Ponniah (2010).
Data Warehousing: Fundamentals for IT Professionals
. Wiley.
William H. Inmon (2005).
Building the Data Warehouse
. Wiley.
